prose:
Property: Dipole moment (Debye)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) via xtb 6.x, density-fit inherent (no Gaussian basis)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (carbon_dioxide.xyz); no further relaxation – single-point electrostatics only.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model invoked).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit electrostatics task → xtb backend.
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged; result JSON contained no warnings; integrity.trustworthy = true.
Reproducibility: command recorded in cli_invocation field; optimized geometry unchanged from input; result JSON at /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpuvtjau1i/live.json

Headline result: the CO₂ dipole moment is 8.0710129e-15 Debye (essentially zero, as expected for a linear, centrosymmetric molecule) at the GFN2-xTB//input geometry level.
